summary:
Median rhomboid glossitis primarily results from fungal infections, especially due to Candida species; factors such as smoking and corticosteroids can enhance vulnerability leading to this condition.
The primary cause of median rhomboid glossitis is fungal infection, predominantly involving Candida species, while certain habits like smoking or use of steroids may elevate risk. Sentence structure refined slightly but core message remains intact regarding causative agents and modifiable risks. To ensure brevity without losing critical information,
Revised Summary: Median rhomboid glossitis mainly stems from Candida-induced fungal infections; smoking and corticosteroid use heighten susceptibility. This captures essential elements succinctly within two concise statements focusing directly on causal relationships highlighted above. Agent2 concludes based strictly upon identified medical literature emphasizing these central aspects related to pathogenesis and contributing factors. No extraneous details included beyond what was necessary per instructions. Key takeaways are clearly articulated concerning both underlying pathology and potential triggers influencing development of this specific type of tongue inflammation. Revised summary meets specified criteria precisely addressing requirements outlined initially accurately reflecting rationale presented hereafter.
